title: Multi-Operator COC roster (operators.roster.json)
description: Schema for .claude/operators.roster.json — the in-repo, committed, per-emitter hash-chained identity substrate for multi-operator COC. Authored by shard A0b-1 (workspaces/multi-operator-coc/02-plans/01-architecture.md §2.1 + §2.3). Consumed by .claude/hooks/lib/roster-schema-validate.js (vendored validator, no external dep per rules/dependencies.md 'Own the Stack'). NOTE: host_role:ci is a valid declared value here, recorded but NEVER advisory-eligible at any gate per architecture §2.3 R5-S-04 — that ineligibility predicate is enforced in shard A0b-2c, NOT in this schema.

$schema.description: Optional self-reference to this schema file for editor auto-validation. Not load-bearing for any consumer — the validator at .claude/hooks/lib/roster-schema-validate.js ignores this field.

repo_owner.description: The verified external GitHub repo-owner login (or org login for repo_owner_kind=org). Architecture §2.3 R5-S-01/R5-S-02. Confirmed at enrollment ceremony (shard A0b-2a) against `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}` — this field is the captured-and-signed result, NOT a roster self-consistency claim. PLACEHOLDER- prefix convention: during a fresh genesis bootstrap (before the ceremony has run), this field MAY carry a `PLACEHOLDER-<intended-owner>` value to signal the binding is reserved-but-unverified. The enrollment ceremony replaces the PLACEHOLDER- value atomically with the verified login. Tooling consuming this field MUST treat any `PLACEHOLDER-`-prefixed value as unenrolled — see .claude/hooks/lib/roster-schema-validate.js::isUnenrolled for the shared predicate.

repo_owner_kind.description: Whether `repo_owner` is an individual GitHub user or an org. Architecture §2.3 R5-S-02: org-owned branch anchors via `gh api orgs/{org}/memberships/{login} .role == "admin"` + root-commit binding.

root_commit.description: The actual root commit SHA verified at enrollment ceremony via `gh api .../commits/{root_commit} .commit.verification.verified == true`. Architecture §2.3 R5-S-01 condition (b).

genesis_generation.description: Monotonic counter incremented on each `genesis-migration` (rule 9c). Architecture §2.2 fold rule 9c + §2.3. Initialized to 0 at original genesis.

provider.description: VCS host the ceremony verifies trust-root facts against (Azure DevOps port). ABSENT ⇒ "github" (backward-compatible default; the .claude/hooks/lib/vcs-provider.js registry resolves absent→github). For "azure-devops": genesis.repo_owner is the ADO organization name, genesis.ado_project is REQUIRED (enforced in code at .claude/hooks/lib/roster-schema-validate.js::_validateProviderIdentity, NOT as a JSON-Schema if/then which the vendored validator does not support), and every enrolled owner person binds via `principal` (Entra UPN) instead of `github_login`. ADO has no commit-signature-verification API, so an ADO genesis anchors via the org-admin (Project Collection Administrators) attestation — repo_owner_kind is "org" and verified is always false; see multi-operator-coordination.md MUST-5 ADO clause.

ado_project.description: Azure DevOps project ref (GUID or URL-safe slug) the coordination repo lives under. REQUIRED when genesis.provider == "azure-devops" (conditional-in-code per _validateProviderIdentity); ignored for github. Interpolated into ADO REST endpoint paths after validation by .claude/hooks/lib/ado-login.js::validateAdoProject (endpoint-injection safety).

persons.description: Map from immutable person_id → person record. Architecture §2.1: person_id is the unit of authority; keys are append-only under a person_id; adding a key or a new person_id is a quorum roster edit (§2.3 + §6.4).

propertyNames.description: person_id keys MUST be a conservative identifier set: leading alphanumeric (NO leading underscore — rejects __proto__ / __defineGetter__ / similar JS prototype-pollution-style keys), then [a-zA-Z0-9._-]*, optionally prefixed with PLACEHOLDER-. Also rejects path-traversal segments (no leading dot/slash, no control characters). The PLACEHOLDER- prefix is the architecture §2.1 convention for unenrolled-but-reserved person_ids (per .claude/operators.roster.README.md) — codified here so the schema is the canonical reference and downstream consumers (eligibility.js, derive-n.js, fold-genesis-anchor.js, recovery-fallback.js) can rely on the shared predicate at .claude/hooks/lib/roster-schema-validate.js::isUnenrolled.

display_id.description: Advisory human-readable handle; carries no authority; collisions are harmless. Architecture §2.1: tooling MUST attribute via verified_id, never display_id.

role.description: Authority tier. Architecture §2.3: role lives ONLY here. Quorum rules (§6.4) match by role + person_id + bound-collaborator-login distinctness.

github_login.description: The GitHub collaborator login this person_id binds to. REQUIRED when genesis.provider is github/absent (conditional-in-code at roster-schema-validate.js::_validateProviderIdentity — relaxed from JSON-Schema `required` so an azure-devops roster binds via `principal` instead; the vendored validator does not support if/then). For owners, distinct-bound-collaborator-login is enforced at owner-add ceremony via `collaborator-distinctness-attestation` (shard A0b-2b).

principal.description: The Entra (AAD) userPrincipalName (`<local>@<domain>`) this person_id binds to under the azure-devops provider — the ADO analogue of `github_login`. REQUIRED when genesis.provider == "azure-devops" (conditional-in-code at roster-schema-validate.js::_validateProviderIdentity); ignored for github. "admin" for an ADO owner is Project Collection Administrators group membership, resolved via the ADO Graph API and bound to this UPN. Compared case-insensitively via .claude/hooks/lib/ado-login.js::principalsEqual (sock-puppet-via-case-mismatch defense, mirroring github_login/loginsEqual).

host_role.description: Whether the signing host is a human or a CI deploy-key. Architecture §2.3 R5-S-04: host_role:ci is AUDIT-ONLY — NEVER eligible to co-sign owner-quorum, distinctness, gate-approval, or genesis/migration records. This schema RECORDS the value; ineligibility is enforced in shard A0b-2c (NOT here).

business_roles.description: OPTIONAL advisory operating-model classification (role-first model; specs/06 §2; ratified journal/0282, INFRA fold journal/0284). Cut by PURPOSE: capability-engineer (≡ agentic-AI-engineer, one role) builds reusable capabilities consumed by a product's RUNTIME (home build); platform-engineer owns loom in full — the ecosystem machine + method/methodology pool + the INFRA team/function — all DEV-PROCESS-consumed (home loom); business-consultant builds specific products on capabilities + use-templates (home use). ADVISORY + capability-scoping ONLY: NEVER quorum-eligible, NEVER consulted by any distinctness/gate predicate, and ORTHOGONAL to both the authority `role` (owner/senior/contributor) and the trust-posture (L1–L5). Optional + additive — absent on pre-model rosters (NOT in `required`). product-owner is NOT a value (the requirements/brief author sits outside the delivery substrate, no signing identity).

keys.description: Append-only list of signing keys for this person_id. Architecture §2.1: keys are append-only; a key is the verified_id (fingerprint).

type.description: Key algorithm class. Architecture §2.3: SSH (default) or GPG; consumed by .claude/hooks/lib/coc-sign.js (shard A0a).

fingerprint.description: The verified_id — fingerprint of the signing key. SHA256:base64 for SSH (case-sensitive); uppercase 40-hex for GPG. The GPG uppercase-40-hex constraint (^[0-9A-F]{40}$, matching `gpg --with-colons` canonical output) is enforced at LOAD time by .claude/hooks/lib/roster-schema-validate.js::_validateGpgFingerprints (#372) — NOT as a JSON-Schema `pattern` here, because the constraint is conditional on `type==gpg` (the vendored validator does not support if/then) and a shared `pattern` would wrongly reject case-sensitive SSH fingerprints. A lowercase/non-40-hex GPG entry fails LOUD at the validation gate rather than silently falling to L2_SUPERVISED at resolution.

pubkey.description: Armored public key — `ssh-ed25519 AAAA…` line for SSH, `-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----` block for GPG. Consumed by coc-sign.js verify() path; this schema does NOT validate the key cryptographically — that is the signing substrate's job.
